title: Micro-CT scanning for post-implant localization of multi-fiber arrays in mouse striatum
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4pnac","text":"We have developed a new micro-fiber array approach capable of chronically measuring and optogenetically manipulating local dynamics across over 100 targeted locations simultaneously in head-fixed and freely moving mice, enabling investigation of cell-type and neurotransmitter-specific signals over arbitrary 3-D volumes . This protocol includes the micro-CT scanning and fiber localization steps. before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9gbhu","text":"Multi-fiber arrays were fabricated and calibrated following the Protocol: Fabrication and calibration of multi-fiber arrays to monitor dopamine release in the mouse striatum. materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2naev","text":"SOLUTIONS:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fvlo6","text":"Preparing Lugol solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e399m","text":"dilute 10mL 100% Lugol’s Solution (Carolina, 10% potassium iodide, 5% iodine) with 30mL deionized water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d0c16","text":"Note: This dilution was chosen to be approximately isotonic to biological tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":82}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inject mice intraperitoneally with 400-500 mg/kg Euthasol (Covetrus Euthanasia III).
After perfusion and decapitation, the lower jaw and front of the skull were removed in order to allow diffusion of solution into the brain while still keeping the implant intact.

Soak brain in 4% paraformaldehyde solution for 24h, rinse three times with 1% PBS, and then transfer to a diluted Lugol’s solution ( see Materials ) to provide tissue contrast for computerized tomography (CT) scanning ( Metscher, B., 2009 ). Initially, samples were soaked in this diluted Lugol’s solution in a foil-wrapped 50mL conical centrifuge tube on an orbital shaker plate for 10-14 days. We have more recently found that using 4 oz specimen cups instead of the 50mL conical centrifuge tubes enables better diffusion of the Lugol’s solution, and adequate contrast can be achieved in three to four days.
After soaking, rinse skulls three times with 1% PBS, and secure them in a modified centrifuge tube.

Image the implanted skulls in a micro-CT scanner (Zeiss Xradia Versa 520, a core instrument of the Boston University Micro-CT and X-ray Microscopy Imaging Facility) with the following parameters: 140kV 10W HE1 filter 0.4X objective 2s exposure time 1001 projections 12-micron voxel size
Register the micro-CT to the Allen Mouse Brain Common Coordinate Framework 3D 10-micron reference atlas ( Wang et al., 2020 ) to bring individual mice into a common coordinate system.
Perfused transcardially with 20mL 1% phosphate buffered saline (PBS, Fisher), followed by 20mL 4% paraformaldehyde in 1% PBS.

Identify and map fibers from the recording tip up to the grid. This process was carried out using FIJI ( https://imagej.net/software/fiji/ ) and MATLAB (Mathworks, version 2020b) using a combination of existing MATLAB functions and custom-written functions and GUIs publicly available in the Multifiber Localization repository on GitHub.
creator: Cláudia C. Mendes
